pages.raw_text:
In addition to Instances cited IN
The Press some time ago comes
a report from Pullman of COM
plants by laborers hired in Spo-
kane by employment agencies and
sent here for work which was not
awaiting them. These laborers pay
81 for their excited jobs and once
away from Spokane don't consider
it worth while to come back for
the money when the Job is not
there.


1t is claimed this sort Of graft is
yielding rich harvest for the en-
ployment agents. Chief Of Police
Waller is appealed to every day
by alleged victims of the game. All
employment agents are under bond
to do business properly, and if
some of the men bilked would re-
turn and bring the matter into the
courts something might be adj
ministered to the agents in the way
of punishment.


The Inland Empire Employment
agency is the one complained of in
the Pullman case.


Hardly had Chief Waller reach.
ed his office this morning when
Robert Movie met him with a COM
plant for himself and two COM
panions who said they had been
buncoed by the Macho Employ
ment company Of 214 Stevens st.
Movie said that he and his two
companions had been sent to Spra-
que, Wash. on payment by each
Of $3.50 The Macho agency guar
anteed them work which was to be
supplied by Pettijohn & Darnell,
sad to be real estate men there.


But there was no work." said
Movie. Those fellows when l got
there told me to hang around
awhile. We hung around all day
Saturday and then we were told
that probably the farmer was not
going to hire us, but that If we
would wait some other farmer
might want US. was out $40 on
the trip besides the fee of $40
CRATER from me by the Macho
agents."


No sooner finished with Movie
than the chief turned to the morn
ing's mail and the first letter open
ed was more about the grating
operations of Spokane employment
agents. All the time a dark scowl
was masking the chiefs face. Fol-
